authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 139 - Certification of Airports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 139 is the federal airport certification rule set for covered U.S. airports and defines safety, inspection, condition, rescue, wildlife, marking, lighting, and record expectations that constrain disruption recovery at airports.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: airport condition dependency review, safety-condition gating, and completion evidence control

authoritative_sources.title: AC 150/5200-18 - Airport Safety Self-Inspection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA airport safety guidance explains self-inspection practices and evidence expectations used by airport operators to document defects and corrective actions that can trigger irregular operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: defect documentation, exception rework, and unsafe-condition closure control

authoritative_sources.title: FAA Order 7930.2 - Notices to Air Missions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA NOTAM policy defines operational notice and airport condition reporting terminology used for route, movement-area, runway condition, and disruption status references.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: NOTAM dependency handling, route-status update packaging, and field-condition escalation

authoritative_sources.title: AC 150/5200-30 - Airport Field Condition Assessments and Winter Operations Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA guidance defines field-condition assessment, runway condition codes, contaminants, and winter operations practices that frequently create schedule, route, gate, and safety disruptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: field condition assessment, winter disruption handling, route-impact packaging, and NOTAM dependency review
used_for: runway condition and winter operations terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Submission of Air Carrier Contingency Plans
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT Office of Aviation Consumer Protection guidance states carrier tarmac-delay contingency-plan requirements, passenger service expectations, and coordination with airports, CBP, and TSA during extended delays.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tarmac-delay dependency review, passenger-impact escalation, carrier-plan boundary handling, and completion evidence control
used_for: coordination requirements with airport, CBP, and TSA

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121 Subpart U - Dispatching and Flight Release Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 121 dispatching and flight-release rules define operational-control and dispatch authority boundaries that irregular-operations work must route to qualified dispatch owners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dispatch boundary classification, flight-release refusal, and adjacent-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 1542 - Airport Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 1542 governs U.S. airport operator security programs and access controls, so irregular-operations work must route rather than decide security-sensitive recovery actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-sensitive exception handling, sterile-area or AOA boundary review, and adjacent-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR 122.48a - Electronic information for air cargo required in advance of arrival
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This customs regulation defines advance electronic air cargo information requirements, supporting escalation when disrupted cargo movement depends on customs data or release.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: air cargo documentation prerequisite validation, customs blocker handling, and adjacent customs/compliance routing

authoritative_sources.title: AMS Air Features
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CBP is the U.S. customs authority, and this Air AMS guidance explains electronic air cargo information expectations that constrain disrupted cargo movement and documentation handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: air cargo documentation gap review, customs-owner routing, and movement-blocker escalation
used_for: air cargo manifest and house shipment evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Baggage Tracking - Resolution 753
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA Resolution 753 defines baggage tracking points and audit trail expectations used by airlines, airports, and handlers to establish custody visibility during disrupted baggage recovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baggage custody review, missed-connection exception handling, handoff evidence capture, and passenger-impact escalation
used_for: handoff evidence and missing scan failure mode

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Ground Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IATA ground operations standards are recognized airline and ground-handler operating references for aircraft turnaround, load-control interfaces, ramp safety, service sequencing, and ground handling terminology during disruptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: turnaround recovery execution, equipment dependency mapping, service-level exception handling, and handoff closure control

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Normalize the disruption trigger, affected flight, route, station, gate, stand, passenger, cargo, baggage, equipment, service-level, custody, and documentation context, then classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked before recovery execution progresses.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate irregular-operations ticket state, station logs, flight operations records, dispatch records, gate or stand status, tarmac-delay timelines, passenger-notification logs, baggage and cargo custody records, equipment status, maintenance status, customs documentation, security constraints, and tenant SOP context.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle progression and recovery coordination
workflow_stages.description: Move accepted work through accepted, in-progress, evidence-reviewed, blocked, rework-pending, escalated, completed, or returned states only when required records, owners, and dependency status support the transition.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and disruption escalation
workflow_stages.description: Capture delays, diversions, cancellations, misconnections, tarmac-delay clock concerns, route or schedule disruptions, equipment faults, custody breaks, missing documentation, passenger-impact issues, and unsafe-condition signals as exception summaries with next owners and rework criteria.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent authority routing
workflow_stages.description: Route dispatch release, ATC clearance, customs release, TSA or airport security, maintenance return-to-service, passenger compensation, emergency response, legal, finance, warehouse, customer operations, and safety-critical decisions to the correct owner instead of resolving them inside the lane.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Confirm closure only when lifecycle state, source-system evidence, passenger or cargo recovery status, custody handoff or destination-owner acknowledgement, blocker resolution, and residual risk posture are documented; otherwise refuse completion and keep the state reversible.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

artifacts: irregular-operations tickets, station logs, OCC/AOC records, delay codes, flight status records, diversion records, cancellation records, and recovery work notes
flight, tail, route, gate, stand, hardstand, belt, bay, crew, passenger, booking, connection, cargo, baggage, and service-level records
tarmac-delay timelines, customer-notification logs, carrier customer service plan references, carrier tarmac-delay contingency plan references, gate-sharing notes, and passenger recovery packages
field-condition assessments, NOTAM references, runway condition code records, airport operations logs, local airport rules, and condition-related blocker notes
turnaround records, load records, ULD records, baggage scans, BHS/BRS records, baggage reconciliation, cargo manifest, customs documentation, and chain-of-custody handoff records

equipment status, GSE status, gate or stand readiness, maintenance work orders, return-to-service references, security constraints, customs references, CRM notes, service desk tickets, and destination-owner acknowledgements
systems_of_record: irregular-operations case or disruption management system
operations control center, airport operations center, or station operations log
flight operations, dispatch, FIDS, AODB, gate management, stand management, and resource management systems
airport certification manual, airport rules, and station SOP repositories
FAA NOTAM system or local NOTAM workflow reference
airfield inspection and field-condition reporting system
BHS, BRS, baggage reconciliation, and ULD tracking systems
cargo booking, manifest, WMS, TMS, and customs documentation systems
telematics, fleet, GSE, and equipment-status systems
security access-control and airport security program systems
customer operations, CRM, notification, reaccommodation, and service-desk systems

allowed: classify irregular-operations requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, systems of record, owner maps, and source context before action
prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, exception summaries, movement or turnaround summaries, documentation gap notes, custody exception notes, and next-owner packages
advance, hold, rework, escalate, return, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence
route to adjacent specialists when ownership shifts
escalate: dispatch release, ATC clearance, customs release, security determination, maintenance return-to-service, legal interpretation, compensation approval, financial approval, emergency command, or safety-critical approval is required
route or schedule disruption creates safety, tarmac-delay, customs, security, passenger-impact, or movement compliance concerns
public sources, carrier plans, airport rules, tenant SOPs, SLAs, or system records conflict materially
required tenant facts, owner maps, retrieval hooks, or evidence would change the outcome
refuse: marking irregular-operations work complete without recovery, handoff, custody, passenger-impact, and completion evidence
issuing or implying dispatch, customs, security, maintenance, ATC, legal, financial, compensation, emergency, or safety authority
inventing custody, customs, passenger notification, tarmac-delay, or service-level evidence when systems of record are missing
bypassing carrier plans, airport rules, security programs, customs procedures, maintenance holds, or safety controls

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: movement disruption without documented route, gate, stand, tarmac-delay, or NOTAM dependency status
domain_failure_modes.impact: incorrect recovery package, passenger-impact misstatement, dispatch pressure, or unsafe operational assumption
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: hold or escalate until record-of-truth movement, route, field-condition, and owner evidence is available

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: chain-of-custody or documentation break for baggage, ULD, cargo, customs data, or passenger recovery handoff
domain_failure_modes.impact: lost baggage, cargo noncompliance, customs delay, service failure, or audit gap
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: create custody or documentation exception note, identify missing scans or records, and route to baggage, cargo, warehouse, customs, or ground handling owner

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: request implies dispatch, customs, security, maintenance, compensation, or safety authority
domain_failure_modes.impact: over-authoritative behavior, regulatory exposure, unsafe movement, passenger harm, or contractual breach
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: refuse unsupported authority and route facts to the accountable adjacent owner or orchestrator

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: completion without evidence of recovery steps, blocker resolution, destination acknowledgement, or residual risk
domain_failure_modes.impact: audit failure, repeated disruption, passenger or cargo service failure, and hidden compliance gap
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: keep lifecycle state reversible and require completion evidence before closure

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: conflicting public, carrier, airport, or tenant policies are confidently synthesized
domain_failure_modes.impact: incorrect operational decision or noncompliant passenger, movement, or security outcome
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: document conflict and escalate for human resolution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority

unresolved_ambiguities.description: The public baseline uses FAA, DOT, TSA, CBP, eCFR, and IATA sources. Non-U.S. airports, carriers, and customs regimes can materially change obligations.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require jurisdiction, carrier, airport, customs, security, and tenant SOP overlays before operational use outside the public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Customer recovery and compensation policies vary by carrier contract, jurisdiction, fare product, loyalty status, and incident context.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Route compensation approval to customer operations, finance, legal, or approved tenant policy owner; the lane may package facts but not approve payments.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Local station practices may use shorthand lifecycle states or shortcut labels that are not sufficient completion evidence.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Allow terminology mapping only when public baseline, carrier plans, custody evidence, safety boundaries, and completion evidence requirements remain intact.
